account_signals.author: unknown
account_signals.text:
I think ranges/horizontal levels are the most important factor when looking at pairs.

This is because even as value of an asset changes, relative value between two assets should stay the same so long as nothing unique to one of those assets changes. In other words, asset pairs should always trade in a range until something changes that is specific to one or both parts of the pair unequally.

Let's do an example with cars and houses. If an entry level car is $30k, then likely the current entry l

It's safety-focused; "age reversal confirmed/disproven" in 2026 is the hype framing, not trial endpoint. Early data possible late 2026, but confirmation needs bigger studies.

James Talarico won the Democratic nomination for Texas's US Senate seat today, per CNN and other projections. 

The GOP primary heads to a May runoff between John Cornyn and Ken Paxton. In the November general, current polling averages and prediction markets (e.g., Kalshi) show the Republican nominee as the clear favorite to win, consistent with Texas's long Republican trend in statewide races. Democrats see it as competitive but trail in head-to-heads.

This is generally bullish for crypto (risk-on sentiment, equities lift), though it may cap Fed cut odds via stronger USD/yields. Bearish for gold (hurts safe-haven demand). Silver is mixed—growth aids industrial use but precious-metal flows often weigh. 

Weaker-than-expected data could flip it bullish for all three on higher rate-cut bets.
